teaching.title: C Pointer Arithmetic
teaching.analogy: Pointer arithmetic is navigating an apartment building — adding 1 moves to the NEXT APARTMENT, not the next byte. C adjusts by sizeof(type) automatically.
teaching.content: p+1 moves one element forward (sizeof(type) bytes). p++ increments. p-q gives element count between pointers. arr[i] == *(arr+i) — defined equivalence. double pointer int **pp stores address of int*.

examples.title: Double Pointer

teaching.funFact: arr[i] is DEFINED as *(arr+i) in C. So i[arr] is valid C! 5[arr] == arr[5] because arr+5 == 5+arr mathematically. 🤯

teaching.title: Linked Lists in C
teaching.analogy: A linked list is a treasure hunt — each item has data and a clue (pointer) to the next. Unlike an array, nodes can be anywhere in memory.
teaching.content: Self-referential struct: has a pointer to the same type. Enables linked lists, trees. Traverse by following ->next until NULL. malloc for each node, free all on cleanup.

examples.title: Singly Linked List

teaching.funFact: Linked lists were invented in 1955 for one of the first AI programs. C pointers make them natural to implement. 🧠

mcq.q: p+1 for int* moves by how many bytes (int=4)?
mcq.explanation: 4 bytes (sizeof(int)). Pointer arithmetic is type-aware — steps by element size.

mcq.q: int **pp. To get the int value, dereference how many times?
mcq.explanation: **pp — first * gives int*, second * gives int. Two dereferences.

mcq.q: Last node in a linked list next pointer should be:
mcq.explanation: NULL signals end of list. Without it, traversal reads beyond the list into random memory.

coding.title: Double deref
coding.hint: **pp follows two pointers to reach x=7.

options: Node never freed — memory leak! Save next, then free: Node*t=head->next;free(head);head=t;
debugging.answer: Node never freed — memory leak! Save next, then free: Node*t=head->next;free(head);head=t;
debugging.hint: Save next BEFORE freeing, then free the current node.

teaching.title: C Preprocessor
teaching.analogy: The preprocessor is a find-and-replace tool running BEFORE the compiler. It has no knowledge of types, scopes, or C syntax — just text substitution.
teaching.content: #define: text substitution constants and macros. #include: paste file. #ifdef/#ifndef/#endif: compile conditionally. Include guards prevent double-inclusion. Always parenthesize macro params!

examples.title: Macros & Conditionals
examples.code:
#define PI 3.14159
#define MAX(a,b) ((a)>(b)?(a):(b))
#define DEBUG

printf("%.2f\n",PI*2); // 6.28
printf("%d\n",MAX(5,9)); // 9

#ifdef DEBUG
  printf("[DEBUG]\n"); // only if DEBUG defined
#endif

examples.title: Include Guards

teaching.funFact: #define SQUARE(x) x*x. SQUARE(1+2) = 1+2*1+2 = 5, not 9! Always wrap params: ((x)*(x)). Classic C trap! 🐛

mcq.q: #define MAX 100. Compiler sees int x=MAX as:
mcq.explanation: Compiler only sees int x=100. MAX is replaced before compilation.
Double Inclusion: Without guards, including same header twice → redefinition errors. Guards skip re-inclusion if already #defined.
5: Expands to 1+2*1+2=5. Missing parentheses! Fix: #define SQ(x) ((x)*(x)).
Conditional Compile: Code inside #ifdef DEBUG only compiled if DEBUG is defined via #define or -DDEBUG compiler flag.

coding.title: Include guard first line
coding.objective: First line of include guard for 'utils.h':

debugging.hint: 2*DOUBLE(3) → 2*3+3=9. Always wrap macro body and params in parens.

teaching.title: ⚠️ FINAL BOSS — THE C MASTER
teaching.analogy: 50 years of systems programming knowledge. printf, malloc, pointers, structs, file I/O, preprocessor — the complete C arsenal. This is your graduation.
teaching.content: Tests ALL C levels: printf format specifiers, arrays, pointers/arithmetic, strings, structs, malloc/free, fopen/fclose, return codes, function pointers, linked list traversal, preprocessor macros.

examples.title: Complete C Arsenal
examples.code:
✅ arr[i] == *(arr+i)
✅ sizeof(arr)/sizeof(arr[0]) — length
✅ int *p=&x; *p=val
✅ int **pp — double pointer
✅ char str[] + strlen/strcmp
✅ struct + . (direct) -> (pointer)
✅ malloc/calloc/free + NULL check
✅ fopen/fclose/fgets/fprintf
✅ return 0=success; perror(errno)
✅ int(*fp)(int) — function pointer
✅ #define — text substitution

teaching.funFact: C runs on Mars rovers, your router, the Linux kernel, and every OS on Earth. Mastering C means you understand how computers ACTUALLY work at the hardware level. 🚀

mcq.explanation: Failure: malloc returns NULL when system can't allocate heap memory. ALWAYS check!
Flush: Without fclose, buffered writes may be LOST. Always fclose after writing!
Undefined Behavior: Freed memory may be reallocated. Reading it is a critical security vulnerability. Set ptr=NULL after free!
11: Expands to 2+3*2+3=2+6+3=11. Missing parens! Safe: #define SQ(x) ((x)*(x))

mcq.q: Linked list traversal: Node*c=head; while(c->next!=NULL)c=c->next; c is:
mcq.explanation: Last Node: Loop stops when c->next==NULL. c = the tail node.

mcq.q: void fn(int*x){*x=99;} int n=5; fn(&n); n=?
mcq.explanation: 99: Passed by pointer. *x=99 modifies the original n.

options: free(c) then c->next reads freed memory. Fix: Node*t=c->next;free(c);c=t;
debugging.answer: free(c) then c->next reads freed memory. Fix: Node*t=c->next;free(c);c=t;
debugging.hint: Save next BEFORE freeing. After free(c), c->next is undefined behavior.
